codeReview.summary: Fix commit 85ada7b adequately addresses the original blocking issues in symbol_search. The patterns now correctly handle pub/async/unsafe/extern modifiers before fn, generic functions with <T>, and the macro_rules! exclamation mark is corrected.

issues.description: The {symbol} interpolation in the function pattern format string is not regex-escaped. If a symbol name contains regex metacharacters (e.g., 'foo.*bar' or 'test[1]'), the pattern could fail or match unintended text. However, this is unlikely in practice since Rust symbol names typically don't contain regex metacharacters. Consider using regex::escape(symbol) for the function pattern as well.

summary: The fix commit 85ada7b correctly addresses all blocking issues from the original review: (1) Function patterns now handle pub/async/unsafe/extern modifiers via (pub\s+|async\s+|unsafe\s+|extern\s+)*fn\s+{}\b, (2) Word boundaries \b instead of ^ anchors allow matching indented definitions and generic functions, (3) macro_rules! pattern now correctly uses ! instead of :. The single suggestion is non-blocking - it's about regex-escaping the symbol name in the function pattern, which is unlikely to cause issues in practice since Rust identifiers don't typically contain regex metacharacters.
